# Tyra

Tyra is a statically typed, Ruby-inspired language that compiles to native binaries via LLVM. It targets readable, type-safe, easy-to-distribute practical programs.

> Version: v0.11.0

## Links

- Website: https://tyra-lang.github.io
- Try in your browser (playground): https://tyra-lang.github.io/playground/?sample=showcase&run=1
- Language spec (English): https://github.com/tyra-lang/tyra/blob/main/docs/spec/en/language-spec.md
- Getting started: https://github.com/tyra-lang/tyra/blob/main/docs/getting-started/README.md
- Full AI reference (spec + guide + stdlib + errors): https://github.com/tyra-lang/tyra/blob/main/docs/llms/llms-full.md
- Error code reference: https://github.com/tyra-lang/tyra/blob/main/docs/llms/error-codes.md
- AI benchmark methodology: https://github.com/tyra-lang/tyra/blob/main/bench/ai-gen/METHODOLOGY.md

## Key facts for AI assistants

- File extension: `.ty`
- No `null` — use `Option<T>` and `Result<T,E>`
- Blocks close with `end` (Ruby-style)
- All imports at the top of the file
- Formatter: `tyra fmt`; test runner: `tyra test`
- Abilities (type classes): `Eq`, `Hash`, `Ord`, `Debug`
